summary: A high‑energy matrix recording that captures an all‑time great Scarlet > Fire transition and Jerry’s incendiary guitar work.
review: The show is widely praised for its fire‑filled second set, especially the legendary Scarlet > Fire segue and a soaring Truckin' → Drums → Space → The Other One stretch. Reviewers love the matrix mix for its vivid crowd ambience, though a few note a volume dip in the second set and occasional track‑title errors. Overall the performance outweighs any technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (≈80%) award 4–5 stars and highlight exceptional playing, especially Jerry’s guitar and the Scarlet > Fire transition; recording quality is praised as vivid matrix sound with only minor volume‑level issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp matrix mix that captures crowd ambience and instrument detail; a few listeners report a noticeable drop in volume during the second set and mismatched track titles from the previous night.

standout_songs: Truckin' > Drums > Space > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows powerfully, with a high‑octane first half and an especially electrifying second set; the only blemish is occasional uneven volume that slightly muddies the later songs.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for incendiary guitar work, dazzling MIDI effects on Scarlet > Fire and Fire solos, and overall 'on fire' performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is described as magical on most songs but criticized for a botched Hey Jude where his playing falters.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive praise for tight, energetic drumming that fuels the Scarlet > Fire transition.
